confirmation_message: Thank you for applying. Thank you for submitting your application to Stripe. Each day we receive hundreds of applications and our teams are committed to reviewing all of them...
confirmation_url: https://stripe.com/jobs/listing/people-project-manager/7953899/apply (Greenhouse embed shows post-submit 'Thank you for applying' page)
human_verification: EMAIL OTP (not reCAPTCHA/hCaptcha/Turnstile). Greenhouse emailed an 8-char security code ('v1D68qVG') to Jamie's own inbox (jamiecheng0103@gmail.com, subject 'Security code for your application to Stripe'). Retrieved via authorized Gmail access, entered into the 8-box security-code field, and resubmitted. Invisible reCAPTCHA Enterprise badge was present but produced NO image/checkbox challenge.

form_answers.plan_to_work_remotely: Yes, I intend to work remotely.
form_answers.most_recent_degree: Master of Science (MS), Applied Organizational Psychology
form_answers.veteran_status: I am not a protected veteran
form_answers.disability_status: No, I do not have a disability and have not had one in the past

notes: JD asks 7+ yrs PM with 2+ in HR (Jamie ~3) — YOE-stretch pushed forward per doctrine (Stripe JD explicitly: 'encouraged to apply even if your experience doesn't precisely match'). H1B cache flags Stripe as historically tech-only for People roles, but that is NOT an explicit no-sponsorship hard stop; the apply form had only a sponsorship QUESTION (answered Yes honestly), no anti-language. Salary range $127.6K-$191.4K posted; no salary field required. Role offers Remote in US (Atlanta/Chicago office or remote 35+ mi), so Portland-remote header is honest.
